name: Martin Sheen
subject: A note from a former president
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Hi Peter. I'm Martin Sheen. I may not be a real-life President, but there are three things I've learned about real-life politics. First: What's happening right now in America is not normal. The rhetoric coming from Washington is hateful and in opposition to the values of inclusion and equality America was built on. Second: To protect the principles we care so deeply about from the extremely partisan atmosphere -- like access to health care, a secure retirement, or investments in education or infrastructure -- we need to keep good lawmakers in place. **Senator Sherrod Brown is one of those lawmakers.** He doesn't just talk about progressive values; he embodies them. He fights for them. He's an authentic leader. Third: **We need to step up.** Never doubt that a group of concerned citizens, however small, can make a big difference. [_**Peter, I'm asking you to help Sherrod. His February deadline is in 3 days, and he needs to reach $250,000. Can you give $5, $10, or more to help him reach that goal?**_](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4d5/4462dc26/48e85112/2000825b/1704377746/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgwMjI1cG0iLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180225pm?refcode=sbe180225pm_m_other&refcode2=3268183") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ **[Express Donate: $5](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4d5/4462dc26/48e85112/2000825a/1704377746/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgwMjI1cG0iLCJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiNSIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJtIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180225pm?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=sbe180225pm_m_5&refcode2=3268183")** **[Express Donate: $10](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4d5/4462dc26/48e85112/2000825a/1704377746/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgwMjI1cG0iLCJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiMTAiLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180225pm?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=sbe180225pm_m_10&refcode2=3268183")** **[Express Donate: $26](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4d5/4462dc26/48e85112/2000825a/1704377746/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgwMjI1cG0iLCJlM3RoYlc5MWJuUjlmUT09IjoiMjYiLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180225pm?express_lane=true&amount=26&refcode=sbe180225pm_m_26&refcode2=3268183")** **[Or donate another amount.](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4d5/4462dc26/48e85112/2000825b/1704377746/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgwMjI1cG0iLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180225pm?refcode=sbe180225pm_m_other&refcode2=3268183")** I've played a lot of roles in my career. A Captain in the Vietnam War. The uncle of a young superhero. A liberal U.S. President battling an opposition Congress. But in real life, I'm a guy from Ohio. I was born in Dayton. My mom was an Irish immigrant and my dad was a factory worker and machine inspector. **Sherrod is one of those rare lawmakers that lives up to the trust we place in him as an elected official.** He puts Ohio families first, last, and always. That's the guy I want representing my family in the Senate. [_**Sherrod is facing tough resistance from right-wing special interest groups -- the Koch network plans to spend $400 million on the midterm elections. This month alone, President Trump threw his support behind Sherrod's likely opponent. If you can, chip in to help Sherrod hit his goal right now.**_](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302fe4d5/4462dc26/48e85112/2000825b/1704377746/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgwMjI1cG0iLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180225pm?refcode=sbe180225pm_m_other&refcode2=3268183") All the best, and thanks for helping Sherrod. Martin

name: Mary Ellen Smith
subject: making sure you saw Bob's email
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate
body:
This is urgent -

I just checked back in with Bob, and he is still concerned that we won't reach this critical fundraising goal. I don't want to cut back on campaign operations, but we're both worried that it'll have to happen unless more supporters step up before our end-of-month deadline.

**We still have a $48,000 budget gap and less than a week to close it. Can you rush a donation of $5 or more right now?**

**Here 's the best way: _[https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6a4b/51dbcc2a/a8dfcf0/3a3017f/4075158566/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDIyNV9mcl9uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180225_fr_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Thanks for helping out,
Mary Ellen

Mary Ellen Smith
Campaign Manager
Bob Casey for Senate

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Bob Casey
Subject: favor, please

Important update for you, it'll just take a minute:

I just got off the phone with my campaign manager, and she told me that we are about $62,000 short of where we need to be at this point in the fundraising quarter. Right now, my staff is debating between cutting our advertising budget, hiring fewer staff, or making smaller investments in our field operation to close that gap.

That worries me, to tell you the truth, because ever since I voted against the GOP's tax scheme, they've really stepped up their attacks against me. I sided with working families, not corporations or billionaires, and national Republicans aren't happy about that. President Trump even endorsed my opponent earlier this month, signaling to his far-right allies that it's time to go all-in on Pennsylvania.

You've probably already heard that Democrats can take back Congress in 2018 and put a stop to the president's dangerous anti-middle-class agenda. But that will not happen if Republicans take over this Senate seat in Pennsylvania, so we need to close our $62,000 budget gap this week.

**Supporters like you are already making a huge difference in this race. It would mean a lot if you could take it a step further and become a campaign donor by chipping in $5 or more by midnight to help keep this race competitive.**

**Here 's the link: _[https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6a4b/51dbcc2a/a8dfcf0/3a3017f/4075158566/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDIyNV9mcl9uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180225_fr_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Thanks,
Bob

name: The White House
subject: Welcome, governors
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=eb4e4baf4e5930b6e2ec063f2bac9f3035b1218ebd6f15307411163978c8f983daf6e16be343ee27c42f5a6688ab295ad08c9b35e72f6bbc

Welcome, governors

This morning, President Donald J. Trump is welcoming about 40 of the Nation’s governors to the White House for a business session covering infrastructure, workforce development, opioids, school safety, and other important issues facing America in the year ahead.

The governors are in town for a winter meeting of the National Governors Association, a bipartisan organization founded in 1908. Thirty-three of the Nation’s governors are Republicans, 16 are Democrats, and one is an independent. Of the 16 Democrats, 11 are expected to join the President today.

The governors will attend 8 working sessions with senior Administration officials, with topics including:

- Improving investment in American infrastructure
- Addressing barriers to workforce development
- Bringing down healthcare costs through better competition
- Driving rural prosperity and growth
- Combating America’s opioid crisis
- Reforming U.S. prisons to reduce repeat offenses
- Keeping our schools safe and protecting children from violent crime

Bonus read:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=eb4e4baf4e5930b69e286d7da62f20565a25ef4888469920dbaa805f1be9b4f1f0ee2806af664400b0d4beedf913412e27072332a168cd2a
Learn how President Trump’s infrastructure plan will boost the role of states.

‘We’ve got to change our way’

President Trump addressed the 2018 Conservative Political Action Conference in Oxon Hill, Maryland, on Friday—one day after Vice President Mike Pence spoke before attendees.

The President and Vice President both covered a range of topics, but one in particular stuck out given its urgency: immigration reform. President Trump has set an early March deadline for Congress to resolve the status of the DACA program as part of a broader package to fix America’s broken immigration system.

“We’ve got to change our way,” the President told CPAC. “I don’t want people coming into this country with a lottery. I want people coming into this country based on merit.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=eb4e4baf4e5930b6784b173a8a1d15834b870ec46d11a9fe3736ed926e51c383cfe2b892fe5fa656db37ae1f215b8ed405e79d97e281bce5
Watch President Trump’s full speech at CPAC.

Why Americans like tax reform

Despite a sustained campaign from the Democratic Party’s liberal wing, a majority of Americans have a positive view of the tax reform law President Trump signed before Christmas, a New York Times poll reports.

The Democratic effort is failing for one simple reason: Results speak louder than words. And the results have been overwhelming. Within 2 months of the law’s signing, more than 400 companies have announced worker bonuses, raises, or 401(k) boosts as a result of tax cuts.

None of this happened because of mandates from Washington. The real power of tax reform is that it lets Americans choose what is most important for their businesses—or their families—to get ahead. The net result could be more than $4,000 annually for the average American family, according to the Council of Economic Advisers.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=eb4e4baf4e5930b6389f4e950b320ed1adcf1d890b9360ace062d7e20e39a4ae3bff20d6c2cecab50f23770855640b29f298110dc5e4aa68
See why more Democrats are starting to break from their party line on tax cuts.

Photo of the Day

First Lady Melania Trump and Mrs. Turnbull of Australia | February 23, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump and Vice President Pence will host the 2018 White House Business Session with our Nation’s governors. The President will then have lunch with the Vice President, the Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue, and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency Scott Pruitt.

In the afternoon, the President will meet with credit union representatives and later with the Chairman of the House Ways and Means Committee Kevin Brady.

This evening, the Vice President and Second Lady will participate in the Indiana Society of Washington, DC’s Tribute to a Hoosier Vice President and Second Lady.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=eb4e4baf4e5930b6e51f8ec80ca2c843a9f7e3b60ea1831cfea25d676a8a6ce1be307758b2edefa79043809ddd10270b446b339c9220dc26
Privacy Policy |

Contact the White House |

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: "work a little harder"
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | An article came out last week, outlining the challenges ahead in this campaign. It pointed out I'm up against "substantial GOP money" and said: _"she will probably have to work a little harder given the amount of cash her challengers are raising."_ I've never had a problem working hard. I first ran for office when I was 25 years old. I was in a six-person primary, up against the boys' club of Florida politics. I knew I wouldn't have the money to out-spend them. So what did I do? _I out-worked them._ Every single day, in the wet heat of the Florida summer, I knocked on doors. I walked through every neighborhood in my district, meeting as many people as I could. By the end of that campaign, I knocked on 25,000 doors -- and won a six-way primary and the general election. I've spent every day since working hard for South Florida families. No matter who attacks me, no matter how much money I'm up against, nothing will deter me from fighting for South Florida's priorities. **Together, we can out-work and out-organize our opponents in this race.** It doesn't take million-dollar checks. All it takes is a dedicated group of individuals chipping in $5 or $10 at a time. When that all adds up, it equals the kind of campaign that wins elections and makes a difference. [_**This month, we need our contributions to add up to $20,000. The deadline is closing in, and I don't want to miss it. So I'm asking you to give whatever you can today -- even if that is just $5 or $10.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f93/622b7b5b/199795e1/307bb1c2/2669950523/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180226?refcode=dwse180226_m&recurring=true&amount=5") If we all work hard, we will win. Thank you for working with me. Debbie

subject: George Soros declares war on Missouri
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
Team,

Please see the below piece from Breitbart News on George Soros' funding of the prosecutor that is going after Governor Greitens. Please share with your friends on Facebook and Twitter.

http://www.breitbart.com/big-government/2018/02/25/hantler-george-soros-declares-war-state-missouri/?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=8f34dde0ee-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-8f34dde0ee-99411629

>From Breitbart News:
February 28, 2018

It wasn’t enough that, years ago, George Soros fanned the flames of anti-police protests in Ferguson, Missouri. Now he’s back to his old antics: bankrolling a prosecutor to go after a popular, conservative Republican Governor, Eric Greitens.

Soros’ Washington-DC based super PAC gave St. Louis Circuit Attorney Kim Gardner (http://www.stltoday.com/news/local/govt-and-politics/st-louis-circuit-attorney-candidate-defends-accepting-super-pac-campaign/article_11036aaf-4b1b-58cd-871f-4084f1ec1485.html?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=8f34dde0ee-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-8f34dde0ee-99411629) almost $200,000 to make sure she carried his water once elected. And she has: She’s softened enforcement of crimes and, worse, has used the powers of her office to recklessly pursue an investigation against the Governor.

That investigation culminated in an indictment this past week—which means that Missouri taxpayers are, yet again, on the hook for spending taxpayer money for frivolous cases, thanks to George Soros.

...

In St. Louis, Soros money is doing exactly what he hoped it would do: St. Louis Circuit Attorney Kim Gardner has proven a train wreck. Her staff has bungled easy-to-win cases. She’s bleeding prosecutors and has been mired in scandal. Worst of all: a judge in Missouri actually had to order her office to stand down on a police shooting case, when it turned out that she was both prosecuting the police officer and determining whether the case should go forward.

And the results are plain for all St. Louisians to see: crime is sky-high. St. Louis came in second last year for the most violent city in America—and the city hit a 21-year-high for homicides. Over 200 people were killed in St. Louis in 2017 because of homicide. And that was in a year in Chicago, Detroit, and New York all saw decreases in total homicides. (If you want to see just how bad things have gotten, take a look at the 10 most chilling St. Louis murder cases of 2017 (https://www.riverfronttimes.com/newsblog/2017/12/29/st-louis-10-most-chilling-crimes-of-2017?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=8f34dde0ee-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-8f34dde0ee-99411629) . All, of course, on Gardner’s watch.)

Any other Circuit Attorney would consider this a personal failure. They would redouble their efforts to pursue justice, go after criminals, and take the fight to gangs. But that doesn’t fit with the George Soros Open Society agenda, and so Kim Garnder has had to take a different tack: She’s gone after a popular sitting Republican Governor. The Governor had an affair, and she’s attempting to turn that personal mistake into a felony charge.

For weeks, Gardner’s been turning over one stone and the next—a lawyer in desperate search of a crime. She hired private investigators, instead of using the police, as is normally done in prosecutions. Why? Because she knew that the police would find precious little to support the indictment she wanted to bring. She’s looked into the Governor’s campaign, his staff’s use of cell phones, and much else—all of which has little to do with the affair. So after a reckless and hasty several week investigation, she’s now brought forward a case she’s almost certain to lose—and she’s subjected the taxpaying public to the worst kind of show trial.

When people wonder why George Soros and his money are a source of such deep concern, they need to look at cases like this and cities like St. Louis. Money talks: and in this case, George Soros’s money has empowered a prosecutor for whom going after political opponents is more important than dealing with crime in the streets. It’s an embarrassment to the Show-Me State, and conservatives around the country need to speak up.

Share on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=http%3A//www.breitbart.com/big-government/2018/02/25/hantler-george-soros-declares-war-state-missouri/&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=8f34dde0ee-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-8f34dde0ee-99411629)
Share on Twitter (https://twitter.com/home?status=George%20Soros%20declares%20War%20on%20Missouri%3A%20http%3A//www.breitbart.com/big-government/2018/02/25/hantler-george-soros-declares-war-state-missouri/%20%23moleg&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=8f34dde0ee-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-8f34dde0ee-99411629)

name: Georgina Cannan, Democrats.org
subject: We're suing Arizona, Derek
body:
This Arizona law vastly disenfranchises people of color who live in rural communities -- especially Native Americans and Latinos.
---
The DNC is suing the state of Arizona, Derek. Let me take a moment to tell you why.

Currently, in Arizona, there is a law that makes it a felony to deliver a mail-in ballot on behalf of another person -- even if it's signed and sealed. As it turns out, this law disproportionately disenfranchises people of color -- especially Latinos across the state and Native Americans who live in rural communities where neighbors rely on neighbors to drop things in the mail for them.

**That's why the DNC is investing more than $2 million in this lawsuit to protect voting rights -- every American should be able to participate in our democracy, no matter where you live. Contribute $3 right now, before Wednesday's end-of-month deadline, to show your support for voting rights and the DNC's vital work in 2018.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfd5/88072077/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/arizona-voter-protection?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfd2/88072077/VEsEAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/arizona-voter-protection?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=26+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfd3/88072077/VEsEAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/arizona-voter-protection?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=27+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfd0/88072077/VEsEDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/arizona-voter-protection?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=28+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfd1/88072077/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/arizona-voter-protection?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfde/88072077/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/arizona-voter-protection?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")
** Republicans insist that Arizona's law will reduce voter fraud -- but there are no instances of fraud due to ballot collection in Arizona. Trust me, I study this sort of thing for a living. The only effect of this law is to limit the ways voters can submit their ballots and make it harder for Arizonans to vote.

Here's the thing, Derek: This effort isn't about Democrats winning elections. It's about making sure that every voter is afforded their constitutional right. Every voter deserves a voice and the opportunity to make sure it is heard at the ballot box.

**[As Democrats, we will not tolerate voter disenfranchisement and will continue to fight against it. Stand with Democrats before Wednesday's deadline by chipping in $3 to ensure all Americans can participate in our elections.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfdf/88072077/VEsFBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/arizona-voter-protection?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=31+-+As+Democrats+we+will+not+tolerate+voter+&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")**

Thanks for your support,

Georgina

Georgina Cannan
Deputy Director and Counsel, Civic Engagement and Voter Protection
Democratic National Committee

P.S. -- If you've ever wondered what kind of work your donation is going toward, this is it. Without your support, we would not be able to take these kinds of concrete actions to protect voters. That's why we need your help today -- if you're able, **[donate $3 now to support the DNC's efforts to make our democracy accessible to all and help elect Democrats nationwide.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfdc/88072077/VEsFBw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/arizona-voter-protection?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=32+-+donate++if+consinConsGroup28497++set+max&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc")**

This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052209f/4067b0a/56cab2377/7e54bfd8/88072077/VEsFAw/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=36+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180226_gc&source=em_20180226_gc&refcode=em_20180226_gc"), or just reply to this email! name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: I can tell you this with 100% certainty
disclaimer_text: paid for by LegitAction, Inc
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

At the end of December, I asked folks to help meet our financial goal
so we could achieve victories in 2018. Within a couple weeks, the sham
Trump-Pence election commission folded under pressure from Legit
members and others.

At the end of January, I asked folks to help meet our financial goal
so we could keep the progress going. Within a couple weeks, notorious
gerrymandering advocate Thomas Brunell withdrew from consideration for
the Census Bureau under pressure from Legit members and others.

Now we’re almost at the end of February, and we need a little extra
push to reach our goal for the month so we can fully fund our

name: Team Jealous
subject: FW: Bernie's message
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
Last week, Senator Bernie Sanders sent an email supporting Ben's gubernatorial campaign.
Ben was proud to support Bernie Sanders and co-chair his campaign in Maryland because Bernie understands that now is not a time for timidity in our country. **[I hope you take the time, read his email, and chip in $3 to Ben's campaign before our end-of-month deadline to show the real strength of this people-powered movement.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a724/7ab0e2c4/7f460891/349a15de/1391887316/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst-bernie18?refcode=OHQ02268A")**
Team Jealous
* * *

Sisters and Brothers -
One of the great lessons of our presidential campaign was that we showed -- in a way that can change politics in America forever -- that candidates don't have to spend all of their time begging millionaires and billionaires for money in order to win.
We proved that if you run a campaign that authentically and unapologetically speaks to the needs of the working people of this country, they will respond overwhelmingly.
My friend **Ben Jealous** is running that kind of campaign as he seeks the Democratic nomination to serve as the next Governor of Maryland. His campaign, like ours, is powered by lots of people giving small amounts of money. He's received more individual donations than any other candidate in the race -- a fact noticed by the special interests likely to spend heavily to stop him. Sound familiar?
**[Will you join Bernie in making a $3 donation to Ben Jealous' campaign to serve as the next Governor of Maryland?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a724/7ab0e2c4/7f460891/349a15de/1391887316/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst-bernie18?refcode=OHQ02268A")**
****[CHIP IN](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a724/7ab0e2c4/7f460891/349a15de/1391887316/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst-bernie18?refcode=OHQ02268A")****

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, [click here](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a724/7ab0e2c4/7f460891/349a15d9/1391887316/VEsC/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/update-your-information?source=emfooter"). Email is one of the most critical tools for our campaign, but you can let us know if you would like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a724/7ab0e2c4/7f460891/349a15d8/1391887316/VEsD/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=emfooter") going forward. If you'd like to [unsubscribe](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a724/7ab0e2c4/7f460891/349a15db/1391887316/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"), you can do so here. [Make a contribution to Ben and Susie here](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a724/7ab0e2c4/7f460891/349a15da/1391887316/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst_contribute?refcode=emfooter"). Contributions or gifts to Friends of Ben Jealous are not tax deductible.

**[>> Donate $12 now <<](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=874b387bdf5a48889e94d1ca155cf431&_e=7kyvRcckyabrSdWUfvhORKYsHSgGiMbWBeMJnzaRy6ql6i0bxA4mDQ5o-RR5EKJjl0OmOOUCm_MvE5UnVAUxVR7Yk_WwD9XIu5Y0zvnIRpa35RnuG_beD-AM4wtfqsSBkful7DxhN_1LCzLTXKglOhe-Fgl9uV1Y4Jkb9IIpNFNTw1RLtBhCyS3Wvum9eFZ8JVxbgsqFF-tZBcvy7ZfruNpCxDQvxiuuy1E1AfToWCbt289GEGiSVpWto8KpwNL5K8wtPfQWr81AzScSQyuZBG54soBtvAi4_ju2bptoEJYRycSUUqF4xpHNVJculcWmXDDjgAyCVVmbEYnnm-H2dbW_jTi4t109DQucu7y-w5rvSRl_iZI7RaYpoB-Bj8nTKPiW1qoD_GsilBe6yxOe40vvRdmRZhIu2gz2ooK96bRhGGXDH2ST7oqDXTmst2jb)** | |

**It’s been a busy month...**

I started out the month filing the paperwork to run for office to represent Indiana’s Sixth District in Congress and now I am officially Ready to Serve, Again!

I spent the latter half of the month traveling all over the Sixth District – from New Castle, to Muncie, to North Vernon – meeting Hoosiers and touring local businesses. **One of the reasons I am running for Congress is to find ways to help Sixth District businesses grow and become more successful.**

I believe President Donald Trump’s America First economic plan is helping our country and our communities right here in Indiana.

_Like our President, I am standing for Hoosiers and America First because it is Making America Great Again._

**[Will you donate $12 to join me in standing for America First?](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=874b387bdf5a48889e94d1ca155cf431&_e=7kyvRcckyabrSdWUfvhORKYsHSgGiMbWBeMJnzaRy6ql6i0bxA4mDQ5o-RR5EKJjl0OmOOUCm_MvE5UnVAUxVR7Yk_WwD9XIu5Y0zvnIRpa35RnuG_beD-AM4wtfqsSBkful7DxhN_1LCzLTXKglOhe-Fgl9uV1Y4Jkb9IIpNFNTw1RLtBhCyS3Wvum9eFZ8JVxbgsqFF-tZBcvy7ZfruNpCxDQvxiuuy1E1AfToWCahTxB3IIsAxP6jm6nIDGUGGEgaESY7lo8KXkw-qk0ihiF6wC7hlHk2Vid86xnRpXycJpdPKgtfncT77IoV_8d1vjZUTy-ZjlV2i8X1Ck0a6GhwEsaWcN9yFnMq17XyKTpW6v11oP_OEuCCfgDQ49TIJSYts7XWF_IInH_gqotwIQPcadyggwcK-TlpuWdsykAx3ivpDt2ryxKovZGeN3FH)**

Now more than ever, President Trump needs fighters in Congress who can get things DONE in Washington and fight alongside him.

_As an unwavering pro-life, pro-Second Amendment, conservative Hoosier, I am a staunch supporter of the Trump-Pence agenda and will fight alongside the President to Make America Great Again._

**[>> It’s time to Make America Great Again – chip in any amount and stand for America First <<](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=874b387bdf5a48889e94d1ca155cf431&_e=7kyvRcckyabrSdWUfvhORKYsHSgGiMbWBeMJnzaRy6ql6i0bxA4mDQ5o-RR5EKJjEjAImfP8LcqwbEm26gQhk31Yd5UiDbuTiY_nDUO1KdW6xpLr8wkXacLe6noaggiRrqibhGHtp_em2cRynGAIu8c98M_Nj6lCIoc5G6JGbCzEqXQqmfMm4Iao5_38xfIr8ytEnvfogiffnmMcWrJRUyyEsY3jh8BrYEllCV2ar2RTEUSsgVMDkmbppy81n6Xwy43drWGYhVlthlSLmzoZhY3aE1Py-tBTLCAvV2eIm6Uzr7CeoANV7wBeqvRfxUprkpmwDf9CN9MQ4nwa71KKVi1pwG9HJVXPRJJ20EK3aWxCYL0H4SOOau60zD5ipIo15gWf1OaEdjmUbgQHMr1-EyNl_SUoiqo2eEraqacht5E%3D)**

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Less Tweeting, More Listening
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Join me in standing against Trump's failed leadership on the gun violence epidemic. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbd/2f5ea88d/7351f3a7/2cbc94f3/3987577488/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156189552568466") Friend, It must be _all_ of our jobs to confront this administration's disastrous agenda. That's exactly what I did today. When President Trump invited me and a group of governors to the White House, I shared the voices of the survivors, teachers, parents, and law enforcement officials who have been speaking up and getting no response from his administration. Look, sometimes leadership can be challenging: You have to make big decisions, you have big responsibilities. But sometimes it's as simple as listening. In the aftermath of Parkland, in the aftermath of 30 mass shootings in the United States in 2018 alone, we're witnessing a horrific failure of leadership. President Trump's solution, arming our teachers, is simply wrong. And if he'd just listen -- to the Parkland high schoolers, to teachers, to law enforcement, to parents -- he'd know that. So I told him that. Speaking to President Trump at the White House today I told him, "We need a little less tweeting, a little more listening." [**Watch my full remarks to the president and share this video.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbd/2f5ea88d/7351f3a7/2cbc94f3/3987577488/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156189552568466") It's not surprising that President Trump doesn't take criticism well. I went to echo the voices of the young people, parents, and teachers across this country who are crying out for safer schools and communities. In the aftermath of Parkland, common-sense gun safety reform shouldn't be a partisan issue. Let me be frank. The vast majority of Americans, both Republicans and Democrats, support common-sense solutions to prevent gun violence, such as expanding background checks, banning bump stocks, and closing the gun show loophole. Instead of tweeting and making dangerous plans to arm teachers, President Trump needs to listen. He needs to hear the calls from the brave student survivors at Parkland and communities across the country -- Never Again. And then he needs to show real leadership. [**I told the president about the people I've been talking to. I told him I hope he'd start listening to them, too.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbd/2f5ea88d/7351f3a7/2cbc94f3/3987577488/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156189552568466") But in the meantime, I'm going to confront him with everything I've got. And I hope you'll join me in this fight. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [WATCH NOW »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbd/2f5ea88d/7351f3a7/2cbc94f3/3987577488/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156189552568466")
---

subject: I know the Koch brothers are no match for you

body:
I'm fighting for re-election in a state that Trump won -- and so are nine of my colleagues. Can we count on your support?
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20180226-Brown-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Team,

I'm fighting for re-election in a state that Trump won -- and so are nine of my colleagues.

And that's not the only thing we have in common: We are all top targets for GOP megadonors like the Koch brothers.

The Kochs have pledged to spend _$400 million_ this year to stop Democrats from taking back Congress. That means attack ad after attack ad -- and my Democratic colleagues and I can't face these attacks alone.

Senate Democrats have a big fundraising deadline Wednesday at midnight and we're counting on grassroots supporters like you. That's why I'm asking:

[**Pitch in $1 right now to the DSCC to defend Senate Democrats like me and give us the resources to take on GOP billionaire megadonors like the Koch brothers.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20180226-Brown-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Donald Trump and his fellow Republicans have spent the past year pushing policies that make life harder for working families while showering benefits on the ultra-wealthy.

Meanwhile, Senate Democrats and I are fighting for seniors who depend on Medicare and Social Security to retire with dignity. We're fighting for parents who have to balance earning a paycheck with taking time off to care for a sick child. We're fighting for workers who hold down two jobs but still struggle to earn a living wage.

But the only way we can continue to fight back is with the help of supporters like you. I believe that right-wing super PACs are no match for the commitment of grassroots Democrats, but now, Senate Democrats and I are counting on you to prove it before Wednesday's deadline.

**Pitch in $1 before midnight Wednesday to give Senate Democrats the resources to take on the GOP super PACs and fight for every state:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/fight-for-every-state**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20180226-Brown-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Thank you,

Sherrod Brown

body:
Important update: Let me tell you something about Rep. Lou Barletta, the far-right candidate whom Trump endorsed in Pennsylvania's Senate race earlier this month.

While he was mayor of Hazleton, Barletta implemented an anti-immigrant ordinance that was so severe and draconian that it was struck down in federal court.

Throughout his political career, Barletta has rubbed elbows with white supremacists, 9/11-truthers, and Holocaust deniers. He's even given interviews to their fringe publications, headlined one of their rallies, and appeared with one of their bands.

In the House, Barletta has voted with President Trump 100% of the time. In the Senate, he will be a rubber-stamp for the most dangerous and hateful parts of the Trump agenda. We can't afford that--and that's why I'm reaching out:

**_[Democrats cannot afford to lose Pennsylvania's Senate seat in 2018. That's why I'm asking you to chip in $5 or whatever you can to re-elect Bob Casey and protect the Senate's firewall against the Trump administration.](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6a55/51dbcc2a/adfa0c2/3a37be2/3521086006/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDIyNl9mcl9uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180226_fr_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: Real reform in the Democratic Party
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
#
Friends,
During the 2016 Democratic Party Convention, over 4,500 delegates unanimously agreed that the Democratic Party needed a transformation in order to regain its mantle as the party of working people.
Whether you were an independent, unaffiliated, nonpartisan, or registered Democrat many of us experienced obstacles to participation during the 2016 presidential primaries. For too long, the Democratic Party has limited participation in their elections through tactics including closed primaries and incredibly strict party affiliation rules. Through collective organizing and action, the Bernie Sanders 2016 delegates shed light on these roadblocks resulting in the Democratic National Committee's first-ever Unity Reform Commission (URC).
**Our Revolution believes that the change within the Democratic Party must occur from the bottom up. That is why we are advocating to increase participation for new voters, nonpartisan voters, and independent voters to participate in presidential primaries and caucuses.**
Larry Cohen, Lucy Flores, Gus Newport, Jane Kleeb, Nomiki Konst, Jeff Weaver, Jim Zogby, and I were appointed by Senator Bernie Sanders to the URC and have been working for months with appointees of Secretary Hillary Clinton and DNC Chair Tom Perez to propose comprehensive Democratic Party reform.
After meetings and debates across the country for almost a year, the URC has presented their final recommendations. Although the Bernie 8 wanted these recommendations to go further, (for instance not just a reduction in superdelegates, but ending superdelegates entirely) these recommendations are a result of a compromise among the entirety of the URC. We believe these recommendations are an important start for us in reclaiming the party of working people.
Among the most important proposed URC reforms are:
* Reducing the number of unpledged superdelegates by 60 percent;
* Democratizing primaries and caucuses with same-day registration and same-day party switching;
* Steps toward more transparency and oversight in DNC spending.

[**Click here to read the full report.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7accd/bed43eb/46838503/75fefff6/542041189/VEsH/ "https://dnc.app.box.com/s/55lk3avwwnpqu2g00sdyd1i6ylz9fz0b")
These recommendations are supported by our partners--the National Nurses United, Democracy for America, Progressive Democrats of America, RootsAction, and Demand Progress--along with DNC Chair Tom Perez and Deputy Chair Keith Ellison.
[**Add your name to urge the DNC members to affirm their support for the URC recommendations and join millions around the country who want real reform in the Democratic Party.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7accd/bed43eb/46838503/75fefff1/542041189/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/tell-all-dnc-members-it-s-time-to-step-up-and-support-party-reform-now-")
****[Add your name](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7accd/bed43eb/46838503/75fefff1/542041189/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/tell-all-dnc-members-it-s-time-to-step-up-and-support-party-reform-now-")****
In solidarity,
Nina Turner

disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www

We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c57a6/24c69fd9/10fd4d661/189725c0/2906372894/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEN53N_digital_email_20180226_EOM598_inact&source=WQEFEN53N_digital_email_20180226_EOM598_inact")

Peter,

A sea change is coming, and my team -- which is responsible for state and local races at EMILY's List -- is working with pro-choice Democratic women who will make it happen.

Since the 2016 election, Democrats have flipped 37 state legislative seats from red to blue, many of them EMILY's List women who won with the support of this community.

Here's how we're going to keep building on that success: We've targeted 598 state legislative seats across the country that we believe we can fill with pro-choice Democratic women this year.

That many seats is a massive undertaking, but massive undertakings are this community's specialty.

**[Donate $3 to help reach our $150,000 End-of-Month goal so we can recruit, endorse, and elect women to our 598 targeted state legislative districts this year.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c57a6/24c69fd9/10fd4d661/189725c1/2906372894/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180225_email_FebruaryEOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+reach+our+150000+EndofM&utm_campaign=WQEFEN53N_digital_email_20180226_EOM598_inact&source=WQEFEN53N_digital_email_20180226_EOM598_inact")**

Already this year, we helped two women flip state legislative seats in Wisconsin and Florida, with one -- Margaret Good in Florida -- representing a 12-point swing from Trump's numbers there in 2016.

From Democrats' upset in the Alabama Senate race to a Kentucky state legislative seat that turned blue last week (after Trump bested Hillary Clinton there by a _49-point margin_), one thing is clear: We need Democrats on the ballot in as many races as possible.

So my team of regional advisers has been hard at work on the ground in cities across the country. They're working with the thousands of women who have already reached out about running for office -- helping them set up their teams, build campaign plans, and start raising money.

Ready to have these women's backs?

**Donate $3 to help reach our $150,000 End-of-Month goal so we can elect women to hundreds of state seats this year:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Reach-February-Goal](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c57a6/24c69fd9/10fd4d661/189725c2/2906372894/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180225_email_FebruaryEOM?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgReachFebruaryGoa&utm_campaign=WQEFEN53N_digital_email_20180226_EOM598_inact&source=WQEFEN53N_digital_email_20180226_EOM598_inact")**

Geri Prado
